---
title: "Week 7 Milestone Factors"
author: "<img src='images/logo.png' width='50' /> <br> Dairy Cow Foot Doc"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    theme: flatly
    df_print: paged
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: false
    code-fold: true
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
  echo: false
embed-resources: true
params:
  event:
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse,
  dtplyr,
  gt,
  arrow,
  rmarkdown,
  lubridate,
  quarto, 
  arrow,
  zoo
  )

events_formatted_all <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/events_all_columns.parquet') |>
  select(source_file_path, ID, BDAT, FDAT, LACT, ARDAT, Event, Date, 
         Protocols, Remark, R, T, B)
         
source('../functions/fxn_milestone7_solution.R')

# create variables for inline code

farm_name <- unique(solution$location_event)

date_max_pull <- max(solution$date_event, na.rm = TRUE)
date_min_pull <- date_max_pull - years(1)


```


```{=html}
<!-- The objective of this milestone is to use the template report you created last week to create another report while learning how to call functions from other places.
Then you will learn the skills from this week to create a graph that takes data from text and date fields. You will use factor skills to order the graph properly to make your point cleaer.
A few notes:  1) I put the code on how to insert an image in the header in the yaml change it to use your logo. 2)Any data (including farm name) that is reported in the text should use an inline text code chunk -->
```

Run this chuck below. It will open the example report in your browser.  You should delete this chunk in your final report

```{r}
#| label: open-html-r
#| eval: false

# 1. Define the file path
file_path <- "images/milestone_7.htm"

# 2. Open the browser
browseURL(file_path)
```


# Report for `r farm_name`

-   This report shows data from the past year from **`r date_min_pull`
    to `r date_max_pull`**.

## Objective

Your objective is to recreate the solution dataset by doing the following with the raw_data table.

-   id_animal is a combination of source_file_path, ID, BDAT
-   Herd is extracted from the source_file_path
extract the protocol data from the text fields
-   protocols1 and remark1 contain the first set of character strings before a non character string is found.
-   convert the exported data fields to real dates

## Your Code Creation

```{r}
#| label: your code

# put your code here to create the parsed variables. name it solution_data

solution_data <- events_formatted_all %>%
  # basename gets rid of file path
  mutate(
    file = basename(source_file_path),
    # str_split removes everthing afer the _
    # Str_sub removes the last 7 characters
    file = str_sub(str_split_i(file, pattern = "_" , i = 1),
                        end = - 7),
    location_event = as.character(
      str_glue("Herd {str_sub(file, 1,5)}")),
    date_birth = mdy(BDAT),
    date_fresh = mdy(FDAT),
    date_archived = mdy(ARDAT),
    date_event = mdy(Date),
    lact_number = as.numeric(LACT),
    id_animal = as.character(str_glue("{file}_{ID}_{BDAT}")),
    protocols_letters1 = str_extract(Protocols, "[A-Za-z]+"),
    remark_letters1 = str_extract(Remark, "[A-Za-z]+"),
  ) |> 
  select(
    id_animal,  date_birth,   date_fresh, lact_number,
    date_archived,  date_event, event = Event,  
    remark_letters1, protocols_letters1, R, T, B, location_event)
  
  
```


```{r}
#| label: create-solution-data

# testing code for problem solving

# solution_test <- solution |> 
#   head(10)
# 
# solution_data_test <- solution_data |> 
#   head(10)

waldo::compare(solution_data, solution)



```
### Create DIM Categories

Then on the new data table.  

-  Create a dim_cat that splits DIM into 6 categories.
-  Order this variable in the correct order so if you were to graph it the categories would make sense and would not be in numerical or alphabetical order

```{r}
#| label: dim-cat-order
#| echo: true

# put your DIM category code here

solution_dim_cat <- solution_data |> 
  mutate(dim_event = as.integer(difftime(date_event, 
                                         date_fresh, units = "days"))) |> 
  mutate(dim_cat = case_when(
      dim_event < 30 ~ "<30",
      dim_event <= 90 ~ "30-90",
      dim_event <= 150 ~ "91-150",
      dim_event <= 210 ~ "151-210",
      dim_event <= 270 ~ "211-270",
      dim_event > 270 ~ ">270",
      .default = NA_character_
    ),
    dim_cat = fct_relevel(
      dim_cat,
      c(
        "<30", "30-90",
        "91-150", "151-210",
        "211-270", ">270"
      )
    )) 

```

### Import Function

Then import this function from Github
This function will create new variables for LAME and TRIM remarks in your data set that now has clean protocol and remark fields and an associated dim_cat variable.
The function is called fxn_code_lesions() and it takes 3 input variables
event_var, protocol_var, remark_var.  

```{r}
#| label: import-functions
#| echo: true

raw_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dairy-Cow-Foot-Doc/os_functions/main/fxn_code_lesions.R"

source(raw_url)

```

### Create New Variables

Then use this function to create new variables for the lesions in your data set.
```{r}
#| label: add-new-variables
#| echo: true

# put your code for recoding the variables here
# 

lame_data <- solution_dim_cat |> 
  filter(event %in% c("LAME", "TRIM")) |>
  fxn_code_lesions(protocol_var = protocols_letters1,
                  remark_var = remark_letters1)


```


## Plot Data
-  Summarize this new data for the past 12 months to create a graph that looks similar to this 

```{r}
#| label: fig-example_lame
#| fig-cap: "Lameness Lesions by DIM Category"
#| echo: true

# this might be helpful for your grap labels.

all_lesions <- c("dd", "footrot", 'wld', "sole_ulcer","injury", 
                             "cork", "other", "hemorrhage",
                             "sole_fracture", "toe_ulcer", "thin",
                             "inf", "noninf", "toe", "axial", "lesion")


# you can use this with the !!! we discussed last week to relabels the factor
  lesion_labels <- c(
    "Digital Dermatitis" = "dd",
    "Footrot" = "footrot",
    "White Line Disease" = "wld",
     "Sole Ulcer" = "sole_ulcer",
    "Hemorrhage" = "hemorrhage",
    "Sole Fracture" = "sole_fracture",
     "Toe Ulcer" = "toe_ulcer",
     "Thin Soles" = "thin",
     "Corkscrew Claw" = "cork",
     "Injury" = "injury",
     "Infectious" = "inf",
     "Non-Infectious" = "noninf",
     "Toe" = "toe",
     "Axial" = "axial",
     "Other Lesions" = "other",
     "Lesion Present" = "lesion"
  )

# create simple function to allow re-use of graphing code
lame_dim_graph <- function(data) {
  
  data |> 
  filter(date_event >= max(date_event) - months(12)) |>
  filter(lact_number>0) |> 
  group_by(location_event, dim_cat) |>
  summarize(across(all_of(all_lesions), \(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE))) |> 
  ungroup () |> 
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(all_lesions), 
               names_to = "lesion", 
               values_to = "count") |> 
  filter(count >0) |>
  mutate(lesion = fct_reorder(lesion, count)) |>
  mutate(lesion = fct_recode(lesion, !!!lesion_labels)) |>
  ggplot()+
  geom_col(aes(y = lesion, x = count, fill = fct_rev(dim_cat)),
           color = "white")+
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(title = "DIM Category",
       x = "Count of Lesions in Lame Events",
       fill = "",
      y = "")+
  scale_fill_viridis_d()+
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 1, reverse = TRUE))+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(
        hjust = 0.5,
        face = "bold", size = 12
      ),
    legend.position = "top",
      legend.box = "vertical",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
}  

lame_plot <- lame_dim_graph(data = lame_data)

lame_plot

  


```


    
# Extension

For this week extension continue to create standard things would want to report

Ideas (or hints for next week include)

start doing math with dates so we can calculate time to event data (cull/repeat)

# Deal with Other Category

```{r}
#| label: extension
#| echo: true

# other category includes abscess and should be recoded as 
# non_infectious

# explore the other category
lame_data_other <- lame_data |>
  filter(other == 1) |>
  group_by(protocols_letters1) |> 
  #group_by(remark_letters1) |> 
  count()

lame_data_fix <- lame_data |> 
  mutate(noninf = case_when(
    other == 1 & str_detect(protocols_letters1, "Ab") ~ 1,
    .default = noninf),
    # remove other catergory if abscess is found
    other = case_when(
      other == 1 & str_detect(protocols_letters1, "Ab") ~ 0,
      .default = other)
    )

lame_plot <- lame_dim_graph(data = lame_data_fix)

lame_plot

```

# Yearly Rates

There's a trick in the reorder to sort by a certain facet

```{r}
#| label: yearly-rates

# to get count of last year
deno_365 <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/denominator_by_lact_group_time_period365.parquet') |> 
  filter(`Lactation Group` == "LACT > 0" & 
           is.na(dop_complete)) |> 
  filter(date_time_period_start >= 
           max(date_time_period_start)- months(12)) |>
  select(location_lact_list, starts_with("date_time"),
         ct_animal_time_periods) |> 
  mutate(ct_animal_time_periods = 
           round(ct_animal_time_periods, 0))

lame_data_365 <- lame_data_fix |> 
  filter(date_event >= max(date_event) - months(24)) |> 
  filter(lact_number>0) |>
  mutate(date_time_period_start = 
           case_when(date_event < max(date_event - months(12))~
                       max(date_event) - months(24),
                     date_event >= max(date_event - months(12))~
                       max(date_event) - months(12)-1,
                     .default = NA_Date_)
         ) |> 
  group_by(location_event, date_time_period_start, dim_cat) |> 
  summarize(across(all_of(all_lesions), \(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE))) |> 
  ungroup () |> 
  left_join(deno_365, 
            by = c("location_event" = "location_lact_list",
                   "date_time_period_start")
            )|> 
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(all_lesions), 
               names_to = "lesion", 
               values_to = "count") |> 
  mutate(risk = round((count / ct_animal_time_periods) * 100, 1)
         )


```

```{r}
#| label: graph-rates


plot <- lame_data_365 |> 
  filter(risk > 0) |>
  # this orders by last year risk
  mutate(lesion = fct_reorder(lesion, if_else(date_time_period_start ==
                                                ymd("2024-10-04"), risk, 
                                                    0),
                              .fun = sum,
                              .desc = FALSE)
         ) |>
  mutate(lesion = fct_recode(lesion, !!!lesion_labels)
         )|>
  ggplot()+
  geom_col(aes(y = lesion, x = risk, fill = fct_rev(dim_cat)),
           color = "white")+
  theme_minimal()+
  labs(title = "DIM Category",
       x = "Yearly Risk per 100 cows",
       fill = "",
      y = "")+
  scale_fill_viridis_d()+
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 1, reverse = TRUE))+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(
        hjust = 0.5,
        face = "bold", size = 12
      ),
    legend.position = "top",
      legend.box = "vertical",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"))+
  facet_wrap(vars(date_time_period_start),
             labeller = labeller(
               date_time_period_start = 
                 c(
                   `2023-10-05` = "Oct 2023 - Oct 2024",
                   `2024-10-04` = "Oct 2024 - Oct 2025"
                 )
             ))

plot

```
